column1: URGENT: Health alert from Jinka, South Omo Zone. We have 8 patients presenting with high-grade fever, severe headache, and jaundice (yellowing of eyes). 3 cases were lab-confirmed as Yellow Fever this morning, while the remaining 5 are considered probable based on clinical alignment. All patients are from the same village near the Omo River.

Konso Health Post: Cluster of 15 cases of acute watery diarrhea and severe vomiting detected in the last 24 hours. Initial investigation suggests a contaminated communal well. 10 cases are currently suspected and 5 are probable cholera. Immediate medical supplies needed.

Turmi Update: 4 individuals in a nomadic group showing signs of high fever and hemorrhagic spots. These are currently suspected cases of an unidentified viral fever. Movement tracking is difficult due to their migratory patterns in the South Omo region.

Arba Minch Surroundings: Local surveillance detected 22 cases of unusual skin lesions and itching among plantation workers. 18 are suspected and 4 have been confirmed by the regional clinic as a localized fungal outbreak exacerbated by recent flooding.
